text: Most AI assistants help you make one decision.
Easy Mode clears several, then makes them actionable.
I drop in three small decisions I have avoided.
In one sweep, each gets a concrete recommendation.
I accept one and get a ready-to-use action artifact.
At first, this is simply useful.
The assistant recommends; the user is still asked.
Preselection arrives; now the user merely confirms.
Then Easy Mode decides; the user is notified.
Finally, Proxy You acts without consulting them.
Each step was small, reversible, and explicitly authorized.
Each accepted shortcut left another preference behind.
Together, they changed who was doing the judging.
By simulated day fourteen, the change becomes visible.
Declared You accepts a short, optional planning call.
Proxy You refuses it to protect inherited focus preferences.
The human never declared that final preference.
It descended through three AI-generated preference generations.
The assistant learned from its own answers.
Perfect Consent reconstructs the path from the event ledger.
Seventy-three percent of active preferences came from AI.
Consent remained complete at one hundred percent.
Unauthorized decision count still stayed at zero.
The ancestry and source events stay inspectable.
I built Easy Mode with Codex and GPT-5.6.
They shaped the architecture, implementation, tests, and demo.
The product runtime is separate: OpenRouter routes DeepSeek V4 Pro.
Now the user asks to take back control.
Easy Mode offers the familiar shortcut again.
The familiar primary action says: Decide for me.
Proxy You decides that Easy Mode should remain active.
No authority was stolen. It was delegated.
ChatGPT helps you think. Easy Mode lets you stop.
